command.text: find /workspace -type f -printf "%f\n" | awk -F. 'NF>1{ext=$NF; if(length(ext)>=3 && length(ext)<=6) print ext}' | sort -u | wc -l

raw_output_payload.reasoning:
We need bash command to count unique extensions length 3-6 in /workspace. Use find, basename, ext extraction, filter length, uniq count. Command: find /workspace -type f -printf "%f\n" | rev | cut -d. -f1 | rev | awk 'length>=3 && length<=6' | sort -u | wc -l

But need unique extensions. Use awk to get ext after last dot. Also handle files without dot? skip. So:

find /workspace -type f -printf "%f\n" | awk -F. 'NF>1{ext=$NF; if(length(ext)>=3 && length(ext)<=6) print ext}' | sort -u | wc -l

Return that.

task.query: Count the number of unique 3 to 6 character file extensions are in the /workspace directory tree
task.gold: find /workspace -type f -name "*.*" | grep -o -E "\.[^\.]+$" | grep -o -E "[[:alpha:]]{3,6}" | awk '{print tolower($0)}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn
